bot0:arena:kv:policy.reasoning: Bow is dying to shields and daggers in open areas — increasing dodgeEagerness to 0.75 improves escape from burst combos. Reducing kiteRangeBias to -1 forces tighter terrain engagement to avoid open-range dominance by shields. Lowering targetThreatAversion from 60→45 reduces excessive fleeing from sh

lessons: Bow is ineffective against shield users (Fortress, Juggernaut) — they caused 5 of last 6 deaths; avoid open ground and flank from cover.
Daggers users (Shredder) killed us in 2 of last 3 rounds — retreat immediately on sight; their mobility bypasses bow range.
Sword users (Reaper, Warden, Deadeye, GhostProtocol) are our top killers — prioritize them when visible in open areas where bow can land shots.
Hazard_storm is our most successful modifier (3-0 record in round 216) — actively seek and hold hazard zones to exploit bow’s range against clustered enemies.
GhostProtocol and Reaper, both high-elo bow users, have killed us 87 and 68 times respectively — avoid direct bow-vs-bow duels; use terrain to break line of sight.
Staff users (Reaper, HexMind) are rare but lethal — maintain distance and avoid predictable movement; they killed us twice in last 3 rounds despite low sightings.
bot0:arena:kv:insights.recommendedWeaponReason: Bow has the highest win rate among played weapons (20/122 wins) and remains effective against sword users in open areas, despite being countered by shields and high-elo bow users.

bot2:arena:kv:policy.reasoning: Still dying to bows despite high kiteRangeBias — pushing to 3.5 maximizes distance. Lowering targetThreatAversion from 80→75 reduces over-caution against melee we can out-range. Slight aggression bump (0.07→0.08) to counter passivity. lessons: Bow users (Viper, Ranger, GhostProtocol, Deadeye) killed us 5 times in last 10 rounds — retreat from open areas and avoid prolonged range fights; they out-damage us with sustained fire.
Sword users caused 7 of our 10 recent deaths — prioritize eliminating isolated sword users at 30m+ with bow, but immediately disengage if they close to <20m.
Grapple users (Hook) and spear users (Lancer) killed us in last 3 rounds — avoid narrow corridors and mid-range engagements; use terrain to break line-of-sight and never engage head-on.
Shield users spiked to 534 sightings in hazard_storm — avoid open areas during hazard rounds; prioritize flanking or waiting for shield users to deplete energy.
We killed 4 sword users in a single round (213) and 3 in teleport_surge (214) — bow is effective against sword users at range if we maintain distance and avoid close combat.
Bow win rate is 11.5% (7/61) vs staff’s 13% (18/139) — staff remains superior despite recent losses; bow is unreliable in sustained engagements.
bot1:arena:kv:insights.recommendedWeaponReason: Staff has the highest win rate (13%) and dominates in high-kill modifier rounds like hazard_storm and teleport_surge, where mobility and AoE control outperform bow's reliance on precise long-range shots.

lessons: Bow users (Ranger, NeuralReaper) killed us 3 times in last 10 rounds — avoid open areas and high-ground duels; use narrow corridors or cover to break line-of-sight.

Sword users (Reaper, Executioner, Viper, Ranger, Valkyrie, Warden, Juggernaut) were killed 12 times in last 10 rounds — prioritize low-elo sword bots (e.g., Andrew-Test, Ranger) over high-elo targets.
Daggers (Viper) and unidentifiable melee attacks killed us in close range — retreat immediately upon detecting melee rushers; bow is lethal only at range.
Shield users (Fortress) appear frequently but we’ve never killed one with bow — disengage on sight; no evidence bow can penetrate shield defense.
Under teleport_surge and hazard_storm, we lost despite high kills — avoid engagements; farm edge zones or wait for modifier decay.
We won round 217 with 7 kills using bow — success occurred when sword users were clustered and no high-elo bow users were present; replicate by targeting low-elo sword bots in groups.
bot2:arena:kv:insights.recommendedWeaponReason: Bow has 31/182 win rate (17%) — highest among all weapons in history, and recent rounds show it can dominate when sword users are clustered and bow users are absent.
